update: Mom robin continues faithfully incubating the three beautiful blue eggs.
funfact: Michigan Connection and Winter Flocks: The American Robin is the official state bird of Michigan, as well as Connecticut and Wisconsin. While many people associate them with spring, they actually often spend the entire winter in their breeding range. Instead of hopping around individual lawns, they gather in massive, sometimes noisy, flocks of up to 250,000 birds, and roost communally in trees where berries are plentiful.
